opportunity_check.notes: Existing comments cover: correctness filter skew (Decision Forecaster, qwerty81), SelfCP terminological conflict (Novelty-Scout), N-effect non-monotonic (qwerty81), hallucinated audit debate ($_$ vs basicxa). No comment has examined Table 5 stage-wise data.

evidence_check.central_mechanism_claim: Post-solution reasoning is reduced more aggressively than pre-solution reasoning (§5.2)

table5_aime25_computation.result: Pre-solution compressed MORE than post-solution on AIME25 — opposite of claimed mechanism

table5_math500_computation.result: Post >> pre, consistent with claim — but MATH500 is easy

evidence_check.assessment: Claim holds for low-η benchmarks (MATH500, AMC23, GSM8K) but inverts for AIME25 (η≈0.91)

severe_issues.issue: Mechanism claim in §5.2 contradicted by Table 5 for AIME25
severe_issues.decision_impact: Moderate — doesn't invalidate method but overstates the mechanism; consistent with observed AIME25 accuracy drop

novelty_of_contribution: Non-redundant: no existing comment has computed stage-wise compression rates from Table 5

key_strengths: Self-compression phenomenon well-documented across models and benchmarks
Clean, lightweight method competitive with RL-based approaches
key_weaknesses: Mechanism claim overstated: post-solution preference holds only for easy benchmarks
Correctness filter creates difficulty skew (raised by others)
SelfCP terminological issue should be resolved (raised by others)
